commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix static game + add skill tree system

- Fixed agents not expanding: units were only on center cell surrounded
  by owned cells, so border scanning never found neutral territory.
  Now units spread across all starting cells and border detection scans
  all owned cells.
- Reprioritized mock AI: expansion first, then attack, build, train,
  then diplomacy (was diplomacy-first which wasted action slots).
- Added 20-skill tree system across 4 categories (Military, Economy,
  Diplomacy, Knowledge) with prerequisites and level progression.
- Agents auto-spend skill points based on their strongest attributes.
- New Skills tab in Agent Detail Modal showing full skill tree per agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clarify platform support: Linux supported today, macOS/Windows coming soon

Users reading the README and quickstart docs could reasonably believe
macOS and Windows produce a working AI stack today. In reality, those
installers only run preflight diagnostics — full runtime support is on
the roadmap (macOS mid-March, Windows end of March 2026).

- Add platform status table to root README and inner README
- Gate macOS/Windows install commands behind "coming soon" labels
- Add "Coming Soon" banner to WINDOWS-QUICKSTART.md (matching EDGE-QUICKSTART pattern)
- Scope QUICKSTART.md to Linux only, remove Windows prerequisites
- Add timeline targets and roadmap to SUPPORT-MATRIX.md
- Update PLATFORM-TRUTH-TABLE.md claims and release language guardrails
- Downgrade Windows from Tier B to Tier C (preflight only, no runtime)

commit.message:
fix(compress): replace in-memory job store with file-based store

In-memory Map is wiped on every Railway restart/redeploy. If the server
restarts between the POST (which stores the job) and the first GET poll,
the job is gone and the user sees 'Job not found or expired.'

New approach: write job state as JSON files to the outputs/ directory.
  setJob(jobId, data) → fs.writeJson('outputs/job-{jobId}.json')
  getJob(jobId)       → fs.readJson('outputs/job-{jobId}.json')

Benefits:
- Survives process restarts within the same Railway deployment session
- Zero new dependencies (uses fs-extra already present)
- Old job files are cleaned up automatically by the 30-min scheduled job
  in index.js (removes outputs files older than 2 hours)

POST handler made async to await setJob before responding, ensuring the
job file exists on disk before the client starts polling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add revision-change badge to BOM Tree Modal

When a BOM tree node's Arena revision differs from the revision stored
in the local rack sheet (col G), a yellow "⟳ Rev changed" badge now
appears next to the revision cell in the modal.

Server: _annotateTreeWithStoredRevisions() walks the fetched BOM tree
and stamps storedRevision onto each node that exists in the rack sheet.
Called from getMultiLevelBOM() after the parallel/recursive BOM fetch.

Client: badge renders when node.storedRevision !== node.revision;
tooltip shows the sheet-stored value for easy comparison.

commit.message:
Fix sidebar: disable hx-boost on nav to use normal page navigation

hx-boost AJAX requests fail with self-signed SSL certificates because
the browser can't show the certificate warning for XHR. Adding
hx-boost="false" on the sidebar nav ensures all navigation links do
full page loads, which work reliably through Traefik reverse proxy.

Fix icon sizes, and fix blitz opponent grid visibility

Sizes:

Real blitz opponent grid:

- AI now shows actual guess animations on opponentGrid (was just resetting silently)

Initial commit: 安全监控平台sec-monitor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ml): production-grade walk-forward pipeline with LightGBM

Replaces the missing ProductionR2Model (never loaded, fell back silently)
with a fully functional, leakage-resistant ML pipeline targeting R² 0.06-0.15 OOS.

New files — deployment_package/backend/core/ml/
  __init__.py         — package entry, exposes run_pipeline
  data_loader.py      — yfinance OHLCV fetch with SPY/QQQ market context
  targets.py          — vol-adjusted 20D forward log-return (y = fwd_ret / rvol_20d)
                        with hard leakage assertion
  features.py         — 16 strictly causal features: momentum (21/63/126d),
                        trend (SMA dist, ADX), vol (rvol 20/60d, ATR%), flow
                        (correct VPT cumsum, OBV, vol z-score), cross-asset (SPY/QQQ/VIX proxy)
  cv.py               — expanding walk-forward splits with 20-period embargo to
                        prevent 20D forward-return label overlap with test set
  evaluate.py         — R², Spearman IC, decile spread, hit rate per fold
  model_registry.py   — joblib save/load with feature schema versioning;
                        ModelRegistry.predict(ticker_df) → raw vol-adj signal
  train.py            — full pipeline: fetch → features → targets → walk-forward CV
                        → LightGBM (lr=0.01, depth=5, early_stopping=50) → save

Modified — ml_service.py
  - Replace broken `from production_r2_model import ProductionR2Model` with
    `from .ml.model_registry import ModelRegistry`
  - __init__: check ModelRegistry.exists() instead of instantiating missing class
  - score_stocks_production_r2(): fetch recent yfinance history per ticker,
    call ModelRegistry.predict(), map vol-adj signal to 0-10 score via
    5 + pred * 2.5 (maps ±2σ → [0,10])
  - Graceful fallback to score_stocks_ml() if model not yet trained

Modified — backend/requirements.txt
  - Add lightgbm>=4.0.0

Run the pipeline to train and save the first real model:
  python -m deployment_package.backend.core.ml.train

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add CLI interface and proper package configuration (Phase 3)

Phase 3 Optimization: Usability + Dependency Management

Key Features:
✅ CLI Interface (mdflow command)
  - generate: Generate workflow from description
  - validate: Validate workflow configuration
  - run: Execute workflow
  - normalize: Normalize LLM output format

✅ Proper Package Configuration
  - pyproject.toml with full metadata
  - Entry point for CLI command
  - Dependency management
  - Development dependencies

✅ Python 3.8 Compatibility
  - Fixed List[] type annotations
  - Added List import to parser.py

Test Results:
✅ CLI help works
✅ CLI validate works (workflow_generator validated successfully)
✅ CLI normalize works (auto-corrects format issues)

Usage Examples:
  mdflow validate workflows/workflow_generator/
  mdflow normalize input.md -o output.md
  mdflow generate "Create workflow" -o output/

Impact:
- No longer need to write Python code to use the system
- Command-line interface for all operations
- Proper Python package structure
- Ready for pip install

commit.message:
Phase 5 UI redesign: app chrome, welcome screen, and bug fixes

Phase 3 — App chrome (AppLayout + ProgressStepper):
- Header and footer: bg-dark-base (#02093A) — unified deep navy chrome
- Header text opacity layers: title white, subtitle white/50, privacy text white/40
- AppLayout: reads currentStep from store; dark steps (welcome) render children
  directly in bg-dark-base main; light steps get max-w-4xl px-6 py-8 wrapper
- ProgressStepper: stays light (bg-white) with 2px gradient bridge line at top
  (from-transparent via-accent-blue/30 to-transparent) to ease the dark→light
  transition; step circles use bg-accent-green / bg-accent-blue; progress bar
  uses bg-accent-blue; text uses text-light-text / text-light-muted tokens

Phase 4 — Welcome screen (WelcomePage):
- Full dark hero on bg-dark-base (set by AppLayout conditional layout)
- Radial blue spotlight bloom from top + 40px CSS grid overlay for depth
- Stats and 'What You'll Get' cards: bg-dark-surface with border-white/8
- Stat values use semantic colors: accent-red / accent-orange / accent-green
- CTA button: linear-gradient accent-blue → accent-blue-dark with hover scale

Bug fixes:
- App.tsx: add aliveRef.current = true at top of init useEffect to fix React
  Strict Mode double-invoke race; previously, the simulated unmount set
  aliveRef to false before initDatabase() resolved on a fresh DB, causing
  setIsInitializing(false) to never fire and permanently sticking the loading
  screen at 10%
- db.ts: add ALTER TABLE migration for schemaVersion column; CREATE TABLE IF
  NOT EXISTS skips existing tables, leaving old DBs without the column and
  causing loadDraft to fail with 'no such column: schemaVersion'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
